title: UK Companies Need More Effective Boards of Directors
content_html:
UK companies need more effective boards of directors

A.

After a number of serious failures of governance (that is, how they are managed at the highest level), companies in Britain, as well as elsewhere, should consider radical changes to their directors’ roles. It is clear that the role of a board director today is not an easy one. Following the 2008 financial meltdown, which resulted in a deeper and more prolonged period of economic downturn than anyone expected, the search for explanations in the many post-mortems of the crisis has meant blame has been spread far and wide. Governments, regulators, central banks and auditors have all been in the frame. The role of bank directors and management and their widely publicised failures have been extensively picked over and examined in reports, inquiries and commentaries.

B.

The knock-on effect of this scrutiny has been to make the governance of companies in general an issue of intense public debate and has significantly increased the pressures on, and the responsibilities of, directors. At the simplest and most practical level, the time involved in fulfilling the demands of a board directorship has increased significantly, calling into question the effectiveness of the classic model of corporate governance by part-time, independent non-executive directors. Where once a board schedule may have consisted of between eight and ten meetings a year, in many companies the number of events requiring board input and decisions has dramatically risen. Furthermore, the amount of reading and preparation required for each meeting is increasing. Agendas can become overloaded and this can mean the time for constructive debate must necessarily be restricted in favour of getting through the business.

C.

Often, board business is devolved to committees in order to cope with the workload, which may be more efficient but can mean that the board as a whole is less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues. It is not uncommon for the audit committee meeting to last longer than the main board meeting itself. Process may take the place of discussion and be at the expense of real collaboration, so that boxes are ticked rather than issues tackled.

D.

A radical solution, which may work for some very large companies whose businesses are extensive and complex, is the professional board, whose members would work up to three or four days a week, supported by their own dedicated staff and advisers. There are obvious risks to this and it would be important to establish clear guidelines for such a board to ensure that it did not step on the toes of management by becoming too engaged in the day-to-day running of the company. Problems of recruitment, remuneration and independence could also arise and this structure would not be appropriate for all companies. However, more professional and better-informed boards would have been particularly appropriate for banks where the executives had access to information that part-time non-executive directors lacked, leaving the latter unable to comprehend or anticipate the 2008 crash.

E.

One of the main criticisms of boards and their directors is that they do not focus sufficiently on longer-term matters of strategy, sustainability and governance, but instead concentrate too much on short-term financial metrics. Regulatory requirements and the structure of the market encourage this behaviour. The tyranny of quarterly reporting can distort board decision-making, as directors have to ‘make the numbers’ every four months to meet the insatiable appetite of the market for more date. This serves to encourage the trading methodology of a certain kind of investor who moves in and out of a stock without engaging in constructive dialogue with the company about strategy or performance, and is simply seeking a short-term financial gain. This effect has been made worse by the changing profile of investors due to the globalisation of capital and the increasing use of automated trading systems. Corporate culture adapts and management teams are largely incentivised to meet financial goals.

F.

Compensation for chief executives has become a combat zone where pitched battles between investors, management and board members are fought, often behind closed doors but increasingly frequently in the full glare of press attention. Many would argue that this is in the interest of transparency and good governance as shareholders use their muscle in the area of pay to pressure boards to remove underperforming chief executives. Their powers to vote down executive remuneration policies increased when binding votes came into force. The chair of the remuneration committee can be an exposed and lonely role, as Alison Carnwath, chair of Barclays Bank’s remuneration committee, found when she had to resign, having been roundly criticised for trying to defend the enormous bonus to be paid to the chief executive; the irony being that she was widely understood to have spoken out against it in the privacy of the committee.

G.

The financial crisis stimulated a debate about the role and purpose of the company and a heightened awareness of corporate ethics. Trust in the corporation has been eroded and academics such as Michael Sandel, in his thoughtful and bestselling book What Money Can’t Buy, are questioning the morality of capitalism and the market economy. Boards of companies in all sectors will need to widen their perspective to encompass these issues and this may involve a realignment of corporate goals. We live in challenging times.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 27–33

Reading Passage has paragraphs A–G. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.

Write the correct number, i–viii, in boxes 27–33 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Many external bodies, held responsible, problems.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn A: "...the search for explanations in the many post-mortems of the crisis has meant blame has been spread far and wide. Governments, regulators, central banks and auditors have all been in the frame."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Blame has been spread far and wide" (Trách nhiệm/sự đổ lỗi bị phân tán khắp nơi) tương ứng với "held responsible for problems".

Các tổ chức như chính phủ, cơ quan quản lý, ngân hàng trung ương và kiểm toán chính là các "external bodies" (cơ quan bên ngoài).

Đáp án: Many external bodies being held responsible for problems

headings: Disputes over financial arrangements regarding senior managers
The impact on companies of being subjected to close examination
The possible need for fundamental change in every area of business
Many external bodies being held responsible for problems
The falling number of board members with broad enough experience
A risk that not all directors take part in solving major problems
Boards not looking far enough ahead
A proposal to change the way the board operates
_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

The impact on companies, subjected to close examination.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "The knock-on effect of this scrutiny has been to make the governance of companies in general an issue of intense public debate and has significantly increased the pressures on, and the responsibilities of, directors."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Scrutiny" (sự kiểm tra, xem xét kỹ lưỡng) đồng nghĩa với "close examination".

Đoạn văn tập trung làm rõ hệ quả, tác động của sự dòm ngó này lên các công ty và giám đốc hành chính (tăng áp lực, tăng số lượng cuộc họp, quá tải tài liệu).

Đáp án: The impact on companies of being subjected to close examination

Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

A risk, not all directors, take part, solving major problems.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "Often, board business is devolved to committees... but can mean that the board as a whole is less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Việc bàn thảo bị đẩy xuống cho các ủy ban nhỏ (committees) dẫn đến rủi ro là toàn thể hội đồng quản trị ("the board as a whole") không tham gia đầy đủ vào việc giải quyết các vấn đề quan trọng nhất ("less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues").

Đáp án: A risk that not all directors take part in solving major problems

Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

A proposal, change, the way the board operates.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn D: "A radical solution... is the professional board, whose members would work up to three or four days a week, supported by their own dedicated staff and advisers."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Tác giả đưa ra một giải pháp mang tính cải cách ("radical solution" = "proposal") thay đổi hoàn toàn cách hoạt động truyền thống sang mô hình hội đồng chuyên nghiệp ("professional board"), làm việc 3-4 ngày/tuần thay vì bán thời gian.

Đáp án: A proposal to change the way the board operates

Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Boards, not looking far enough ahead.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn E: "One of the main criticisms of boards... is that they do not focus sufficiently on longer-term matters of strategy, sustainability... but instead concentrate too much on short-term financial metrics."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Do not focus sufficiently on longer-term matters" (Không tập trung đủ vào các vấn đề dài hạn) đồng nghĩa với việc "not looking far enough ahead" (Không nhìn đủ xa về tương lai).

Lưu ý: Chữ "Broads" trong ảnh nhãn kéo thả của bạn là lỗi chính tả viết nhầm của hệ thống web, từ gốc đúng phải là "Boards".

Đáp án: Broads not looking far enough ahead

Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Disputes, financial arrangements, senior managers.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn F: "Compensation for chief executives has become a combat zone where pitched battles between investors, management and board members are fought..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Chief executives" chính là các nhà quản lý cấp cao ("senior managers").

"Compensation" (Thù lao, lương thưởng) đồng nghĩa với "financial arrangements".

"Combat zone / pitched battles" (Trận chiến nảy lửa) đồng nghĩa với các tranh chấp, bất đồng ("disputes").

Đáp án: Disputes over financial arrangements regarding senior managers

Bước 1: Keyword trong tiêu đề

Possible need, fundamental change, every area of business.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn G: "Boards of companies in all sectors will need to widen their perspective to encompass these issues and this may involve a realignment of corporate goals."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"All sectors" đồng nghĩa với "every area of business".

"Realignment of corporate goals" (Sắp xếp/định hình lại toàn bộ mục tiêu doanh nghiệp) tương ứng với một sự thay đổi cơ bản, gốc rễ ("fundamental change").

Đáp án: The possible need for fundamental change in every area of business

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 34–37

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 34–37 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Close scrutiny, behavior of boards, increased, since economic downturn.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Following the 2008 financial meltdown... The knock-on effect of this scrutiny has been to make the governance of companies in general an issue of intense public debate and has significantly increased the pressures on... directors."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Financial meltdown" chính là cuộc khủng hoảng/suy thoái kinh tế ("economic downturn"). Sau sự kiện này, việc giám sát ("scrutiny") đối với ban quản trị đã tăng mạnh tạo nên các làn sóng tranh luận công khai. Thông tin trùng khớp hoàn toàn.

Đáp án: YES

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: Close scrutiny of the behaviour of boards has increased since the economic downturn.

questions.explanation:
Banks have been mismanaged to a greater extent than other businesses.

Bước 1: Keyword

Banks, mismanaged, greater extent, other businesses.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn A: "The role of bank directors and management and their widely publicised failures have been extensively picked over and examined..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc nêu rõ sai lầm của các ngân hàng bị đem ra mổ xẻ và kiểm tra rất kỹ, nhưng không hề có bất kỳ sự so sánh nào cho thấy ngân hàng bị quản lý tồi tệ "hơn" (to a greater extent) các loại hình doanh nghiệp khác.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Banks have been mismanaged to a greater extent than other businesses.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Board meetings, continue for as long as necessary, debate in full.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Agendas can become overloaded and this can mean the time for constructive debate must necessarily be restricted in favour of getting through the business."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc nói rằng do chương trình nghị sự bị quá tải nên thời gian dành cho tranh luận mang tính xây dựng bắt buộc phải bị giới hạn, thu hẹp lại ("necessarily be restricted") để giải quyết cho xong sự vụ.

Điều này trái ngược hoàn toàn với ý kiến cho rằng cuộc họp kéo dài "đủ lâu đến khi nào cần thiết để tranh luận đầy đủ".

Đáp án: NO
questions.question_text: Board meetings normally continue for as long as necessary to debate matters in full.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Committee structure, ensure, fully informed, significant issues.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "Often, board business is devolved to committees... but can mean that the board as a whole is less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc khẳng định việc đẩy việc cho các ủy ban (committee structure) có thể khiến cho hội đồng quản trị ít được tham gia/ít nắm rõ các vấn đề quan trọng hơn ("less involved in fully addressing some of the most important issues").

Nhận định này trái ngược với từ "ensure... fully informed" (đảm bảo được cung cấp thông tin đầy đủ) của câu hỏi.

Đáp án: NO
questions.question_text: Using a committee structure would ensure that board members are fully informed about significant issues.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 38–40

Complete the sentences below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Before 2008, non-executive directors, disadvantage, lack of.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn D: "...boards would have been particularly appropriate for banks where the executives had access to information that part-time non-executive directors lacked, leaving the latter unable to comprehend or anticipate the 2008 crash."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Lacked" đồng nghĩa với "lack of". Trước năm 2008, các giám đốc không điều hành (non-executive directors) lâm vào thế bất lợi và không thể lường trước khủng hoảng vì họ thiếu thông tin ("information") mà các giám đốc điều hành có.

Đáp án: information

_blockConfig.noteContent:
- Before 2008, non-executive directors were at a disadvantage because of their lack of [38].
- Boards tend to place too much emphasis on [39] considerations that are only of short-term relevance.
- On certain matters, such as pay, the board may have to accept the views of [40]
sentenceItems: • Before 2008, non-executive directors were at a disadvantage because of their lack of [38].
• Boards tend to place too much emphasis on [39] considerations that are only of short-term relevance.
• On certain matters, such as pay, the board may have to accept the views of [40]

questions.questionHtml:
• Before 2008, non-executive directors were at a disadvantage because of their lack of 38_______.

• Boards tend to place too much emphasis on 39_______ considerations that are only of short-term relevance.

• On certain matters, such as pay, the board may have to accept the views of 40_______
questions.question_text: Before 2008, non-executive directors were at a disadvantage because of their lack of __________.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Tend to, place too much emphasis on, short-term relevance.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn E: "...but instead concentrate too much on short-term financial metrics."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Place too much emphasis on" và "concentrate too much on" đồng nghĩa với nhau.

Khía cạnh ngắn hạn mà các hội đồng tập trung quá mức vào chính là các chỉ số/yếu tố thuộc về tài chính ("financial").

Đáp án: financial
questions.question_text: Boards tend to place too much emphasis on ___________ considerations that are only of short-term relevance.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
“accept the views of”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn F:
“shareholders use their muscle in the area of pay to pressure boards”
và
“Their powers to vote down executive remuneration policies increased”

Bước 3: Phân tích
“shareholders” là những người gây áp lực lên hội đồng quản trị về vấn đề lương thưởng.
Trong đáp án, “shareholders” được paraphrase thành investors.

=> Đáp án: investors
questions.question_text: On certain matters, such as pay, the board may have to accept the views of __________.
